statement.focus_question: Estimate $f(N)$. In particular, is it true that $f(N)=(\tfrac{1}{2}+o(1))N$?
statement.normalized: Let $f(N)$ be the size of the largest $A\subseteq \{1,\ldots,N\}$ such that there are no solutions to\[\frac{1}{a}= \frac{1}{b_1}+\cdots+\frac{1}{b_k}\]with distinct $a,b_1,\ldots,b_k\in A$? Estimate $f(N)$. In particular, is it true that $f(N)=(\tfrac{1}{2}+o(1))N$?
statement.original:
Let $f(N)$ be the size of the largest $A\subseteq \{1,\ldots,N\}$ such that there are no solutions to\[\frac{1}{a}= \frac{1}{b_1}+\cdots+\frac{1}{b_k}\]with distinct $a,b_1,\ldots,b_k\in A$?

Estimate $f(N)$. In particular, is it true that $f(N)=(\tfrac{1}{2}+o(1))N$?
